steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The target word appears in the line "The keenest vision can't define;" The surrounding context describes the border between a leaf and its reflection as perfectly blended and imperceptible, so "define" means to make out or perceive that boundary.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Perceive’: Correct. The sharpest sight cannot perceive (make out) the border line because the blending is so perfect. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Abbreviate’: Wrong. "Abbreviate" means to shorten, but the line is about whether vision can make out a boundary. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Restate’: Wrong. "Restate" means to say again in different words; the poem is about seeing, not rephrasing. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Explain’: Wrong. "Explain" means to make something clear in words; the line says even the sharpest vision cannot detect the border. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
